steps.title: Use the evidence contrast
steps.content: The sentence contrasts randomized clinical trials with practitioners' real-world conclusions. Because practitioners may overlook confounding variables, trial results may fail to support or confirm those conclusions.

steps.title: Choose confirmation
steps.content: Choice A means bypass, choice C means spread widely, and choice D means put into effect. "Corroborate" means confirm or support with evidence, which fits trial results failing to back up observational conclusions. The answer is B.
